description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Refined Den dashboard and organization flows, including smoother onboarding, cleaner worker connect actions, and reduced polling jank.
- Improved desktop session UX with compact conversation quick action, persistent default model handling, and clearer update badge visibility.
Fixes & polish
- Stabilized remote workspace binding and OpenWork link token handling across app and Den surfaces.
- Tuned app cloud settings and share template inputs to better match the latest UI direction.
- Added local-host workspace creation reliability improvements.
Packaging / ops
- Bumped all package versions to 0.11.195, refreshed lockfiles, and updated AUR packaging metadata.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.194...v0.11.195
